stemHtml: A Direct (Sector 3) entry procedure to a holding pattern is described in ICAO Doc 8168 as follows: 'Sector 3 procedure (direct entry): Having reached the fix, the aircraft is turned right to follow the holding pattern.' If a holding pattern uses an NDB as the fix, the aircraft will need to fly directly towards, and then overfly, the NDB before commencing a turn. Regardless of the type of ADF, what indications will the pilot have for when to commence this turn?

options.a: The NDB distance indicator will indicate a distance of 0 NM, depending on the altitude when passing overhead.
options.b: The head of the needle will point approximately straight down when flying TO the fix and then rotate through 180° when passing overhead.
options.c: The needle will indicate the appropriate course and the TO/FROM indicator will change to a FROM indication when passing overhead.
options.d: The head of the needle will point approximately straight up when flying TO the fix and then rotate through 180° when passing overhead.
